description: This figure characterizes the microstructure, elemental distribution, and tensile mechanical properties of Fe-30Mn-8Al-1C low-density steel processed by 1000 °C/15 min recrystallization annealing followed by 550 °C/2 h aging. Subfigure (a) presents SEM secondary electron (SEI), backscattered electron (COMPO) micrographs (20 μm scale bar) and corresponding EDS elemental mappings of Fe, Mn, Al, and C, showing all four elements are uniformly distributed in the matrix without obvious segregation. Subfigure (b) is the engineering stress-strain curve, exhibiting a yield strength of ~970 MPa, ultimate tensile strength of ~1150 MPa, and total elongation of ~40%, indicating excellent strength-ductility synergy. Subfigure (c) shows an optical micrograph (50 μm scale bar) revealing uniform polygonal recrystallized austenite grains. These results confirm the designed steel has expected homogeneous microstructure and good mechanical performance, matching machine learning predictions for the FeMnAlC system.
